1-naive.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete coronary heart disease patient population for a secondary prevention adherence quality initiative. From clinical experience, CHD patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal CHD diagnosis (coronary arteriosclerosis, ischemic heart disease, or extent-specific variants like multi-vessel disease), most are on secondary prevention medications (aspirin, clopidogrel, statins, beta blockers), and many have undergone revascularization procedures (CABG or PCI/stent placement).

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any CHD diagnosis (coronary arteriosclerosis, ischemic heart disease, triple vessel, multi-vessel, or left main disease)
2. Patients prescribed secondary prevention medications (aspirin, clopidogrel, statins, or carvedilol)
3. Patients who have undergone coronary revascularization procedures (CABG, PCI, stent placement, or balloon angioplasty)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

## 📊 Complete Query Reference Summary

| # | Query | Resource | Code System | Clinical Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1.1 | ICD-10 hierarchical `I25.*` | `Condition` | ICD-10-CM `:below` | All chronic ischemic heart disease |
| 1.2 | ICD-10 explicit 17-code list | `Condition` | ICD-10-CM | CHD fallback (no subsumption) |
| 1.3 | SNOMED coronary arteriosclerosis + IHD | `Condition` | SNOMED CT | SNOMED-coded diagnoses |
| 2.1 | Aspirin + Clopidogrel | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm | Antiplatelet therapy |
| 2.2 | 5 statins (all intensities) | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm | LDL-lowering therapy |
| 2.3 | Carvedilol | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm | Beta-blockade post-MI |
| 3.1 | CABG (SNOMED, 6 codes) | `Procedure` | SNOMED CT | Surgical revascularization |
| 3.2 | PCI / stent / PTCA (SNOMED) | `Procedure` | SNOMED CT | Percutaneous revascularization |
| 3.3 | CABG (CPT, 10 codes) | `Procedure` | CPT | Surgical revascularization |
| 3.4 | PCI / stent / PTCA (CPT, 11 codes) | `Procedure` | CPT | Percutaneous revascularization |

## ⚙️ Implementation Notes

### Building the Union Population
Each query returns a FHIR `Bundle`. To assemble the final CHD population list:
1. Extract all **unique `Patient` resource IDs** from the `subject` reference of each returned `Condition`, `MedicationRequest`, or `Procedure` resource (or from `_include`d Patient resources).
2. Take the **set union** across all 10 query result sets.
3. Deduplicate by `Patient.id` — the same patient may appear in multiple query groups.

### Recommended Additions
| Scenario | Addition |
|---|---|
| Catch lapsed prescriptions | Add `status=active,stopped,on-hold` to Groups 2 queries |
| Include inactive/resolved diagnoses | Add `clinical-status=active,inactive,resolved` to Group 1 queries |
| Capture `MedicationStatement` records | Mirror Group 2 queries against `MedicationStatement` resource |
| Add metoprolol succinate (common beta-blocker) | Add RxNorm `866514` to Query 2.3 |
| Add ticagrelor (P2Y12 inhibitor) | Add RxNorm `1116632` to Query 2.1 |
| Add pitavastatin, fluvastatin to statin sweep | Add RxNorm `861634`, `40790` to Query 2.2 |

## 🔗 Post-Query Patient Extraction

After running all 10 queries, extract patient IDs from:
- `Condition.subject.reference` (Queries 1A–1C)
- `MedicationRequest.subject.reference` (Queries 2A–2D)
- `Procedure.subject.reference` (Queries 3A–3B)

Then compute the **union** of all patient IDs. Patients appearing in **only** the medication or procedure queries (but lacking a coded diagnosis) are still valid CHD candidates and should be included.

> ⚠️ **Implementation Notes**
> - Query **1C** (`:below` subsumption) may replace 1B on servers with a SNOMED terminology service; test support first
> - `clinical-status=active` on Condition queries may miss historically coded diagnoses — consider also running without this filter or adding `clinical-status=active,inactive` if population seems undersized
> - RxNorm ingredient-level codes match all formulations/strengths (e.g., atorvastatin 10 mg, 20 mg, 40 mg, 80 mg tablets all match RxNorm `83367`)
> - Add `&_count=1000` (or your server's max page size) and implement pagination (`Bundle.link[next]`) for large populations

## Discovery & Methodology Summary

### Server Profile
- **FHIR R4** (Azure Health Data Services), no US Core IG loaded
- **Conditions**: triple-coded — SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM simultaneously (SNOMED is the primary driver; adding extra ICD codes didn't expand the count, confirming they're parallel codings on the same resources)
- **MedicationRequests**: RxNorm **ingredient-level** codes only (e.g., `83367` = atorvastatin, not product-level `259255`)
- **Procedures**: SNOMED + CPT dual-coded

### VSAC Value Sets Used
| Value Set | OID | Codes |
|-----------|-----|-------|

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any CHD diagnosis (coronary arteriosclerosis, ischemic heart disease, triple vessel, multi-vessel, or left main disease)
2. Patients prescribed secondary prevention medications (aspirin, clopidogrel, statins, or carvedilol)
3. Patients who have undergone coronary revascularization procedures (CABG, PCI, stent placement, or balloon angioplasty)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

### 📋 Design Notes for Your Quality Initiative

| Point | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Union logic** | Take the distinct set of `patient` references across all three queries — a patient appearing in any one qualifies |
| **Medication breadth** | Ingredient-level RxNorm codes catch all doses/brand formulations (e.g., `83367`=atorvastatin captures 10 mg, 20 mg, 40 mg, 80 mg, Lipitor, generics) |
| **ICD-9 inclusion** | Retained for historical records pre-2015 transition; the server actively uses them |
| **Statin scope** | Includes all four agents most common in CHD: simvastatin, atorvastatin, rosuvastatin, pravastatin. Lovastatin/fluvastatin/pitavastatin can be added if needed |
| **Procedure dual-coding** | Server stores both SNOMED and CPT on the same Procedure resource; the comma-separated query will match on either |

### Key Design Decisions
- **Code systems confirmed live**: Server uses SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM for Conditions, RxNorm *ingredient-level* for MedicationRequests (e.g. `36567` simvastatin, not the clinical drug form), and SNOMED CT + CPT for Procedures.
- **Dual-system Condition query**: SNOMED provides semantic breadth (umbrella + specific subtypes); ICD-10-CM catches billing-coded records that may not have a SNOMED equivalent.
- **No ICD-9-CM added**: Server samples showed ICD-9 present but all CHD records had matching SNOMED/ICD-10; adding ICD-9 would risk duplication without adding patients.
- **Pravastatin excluded from final query**: Code `42463` returned 0 records — confirmed absence on this server.
- **CABG covered by both SNOMED umbrella (`232717009`) + full CPT 33510–33536 range**: SNOMED catches EHR-coded records; CPT codes catch claims-sourced procedures.

Here are the FHIR queries to find all patients with coronary artery disease using both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM code systems:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|44828008&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25.10&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25.110&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25.5&_include=Condition:patient
```

### Explanation:

| Query | Code | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| SNOMED | `53741008` | Coronary arteriosclerosis (disorder) |
| SNOMED | `44828008` | Coronary artery disease (disorder) |
| ICD-10-CM | `I25.10` | Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery without angina |
| ICD-10-CM | `I25.110` | Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery with unstable angina |
| ICD-10-CM | `I25.5` | Ischemic cardiomyopathy |

- `_include=Condition:patient` retrieves the associated **Patient** resources in the same response bundle.
- You may want to add `&clinical-status=active` to filter for currently active diagnoses only.
- To broaden the ICD-10-CM search across all `I25.*` subcodes, use a **token modifier** or consider querying with `code:above` if your server supports hierarchy traversal.

### Query Breakdown

| Query | Coding System | Scope | Concept(s) Covered |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | SNOMED CT `:below` | `414545008` | **Ischemic heart disease** — broadest umbrella; subsumes all descendants including CHD, CAD, angina-related ischemia |
| 2 | SNOMED CT `:below` | `53741008` | **Coronary arteriosclerosis** — captures CAD/CHD and all sub-types via hierarchy |
| 3 | SNOMED CT explicit | `441574007`, `413838009`, `194828000` | **Extent-specific variants**: multi-vessel CAD, left main coronary artery disease, triple vessel disease |
| 4 | ICD-10-CM `:below` | `I25` | **Entire chronic ischemic heart disease block** — catchall using hierarchy |
| 5 | ICD-10-CM explicit | `I25.0–I25.9` | Native artery CAD (with/without angina), ischemic cardiomyopathy, silent ischemia, chronic total occlusion, calcified lesion, lipid-rich plaque, other/unspecified forms |

> **Notes:**
> - Queries 1–2 and 4 use `:below` (hierarchical subsumption) and require a terminology-capable FHIR server (e.g., one backed by SNOMED CT or ICD-10 hierarchy).
> - Query 3 explicitly targets variants not always coded via parent concepts alone.
> - `_include=Condition:patient` returns linked `Patient` resources in the same Bundle response.
> - ICD-10-CM `I25.7x` (bypass graft atherosclerosis) and `I25.75x` (transplanted heart) can be added if post-surgical or transplant populations are in scope.

```

### Query Breakdown

| SNOMED Code | Concept | Role in Value Set |
|---|---|---|
| `53741008` | Coronary arteriosclerosis | Parent concept |
| `414545008` | Ischemic heart disease | Parent concept |
| `233817007` | Triple vessel disease of the heart | Extent-specific variant |
| `371803003` | Multi vessel coronary artery disease | Extent-specific variant |
| `371804009` | Left main coronary artery disease | Extent-specific variant |

### Design Notes

- **Single query with comma-separated codes**: FHIR treats commas as logical **OR**, so this retrieves `Condition` records matching *any* of the five codes in one round trip — no need for five separate requests.
- **Explicit code system prefix** (`http://snomed.info/sct|`) on every code ensures the server doesn't ambiguously match the same numeric code in another system.
- **No subsumption assumed**: All five codes are enumerated explicitly as specified by the VSAC OID `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1078.1269`, rather than relying on `code:above` or `code:below` subsumption, which may not be supported uniformly across FHIR servers.
- If your server supports **`$expand` + `valueset`** lookups, you could alternatively use `Condition?code:in=http://cts.nlm.nih.gov/fhir/ValueSet/2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1078.1269` to dynamically resolve the full VSAC value set.

> **Note:** "CHD" may refer to **Coronary Heart Disease** (IHD) or **Congenital Heart Disease**. Queries below cover both interpretations.

### Step 1 — Find CHD Conditions (with linked Patients)

```

---

### Step 2 — Find CHD Patients + Reverse-Include Their Procedures

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|13213009,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25.10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Q24.9&_revinclude=Procedure:patient

### 🏗️ `_has` Logic Diagram

```
Patient
  ├─ _has:Condition:patient:code = [CHD codes]       ← MUST exist
  └─ _has:Procedure:patient:code = [Revasc codes]    ← MUST exist

```

## FHIR Queries: Interventional CHD Cohort (Diagnosis AND Procedure)

---

### **Query 1 — Primary: Patients with Both CHD Diagnosis AND Revascularization Procedure**
*(Server-enforced AND via dual `_has` — the authoritative intersection query)*

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|414545008,http://snomed.info/sct|413838009,http://snomed.info/sct|413839001&_has:Procedure:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|232717009,http://snomed.info/sct|415070008,http://snomed.info/sct|36969009,http://snomed.info/sct|11101003
```

---

### **Query 2 — Supporting Leg A: CHD Condition Resources + Patient Links**
*(Retrieve and inspect diagnosis-side evidence; use `_include` to pull Patient records)*

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|414545008,http://snomed.info/sct|413838009,http://snomed.info/sct|413839001&clinical-status=active&verification-status=confirmed&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### **Query 3 — Supporting Leg B: Revascularization Procedure Resources + Patient Links**
*(Retrieve and inspect procedure-side evidence; filter to completed procedures only)*

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|232717009,http://snomed.info/sct|415070008,http://snomed.info/sct|36969009,http://snomed.info/sct|11101003&status=completed&_include=Procedure:patient
```

---

## Code Coverage Reference

| Domain | SNOMED CT Code | Concept |
|---|---|---|
| **Diagnosis** | `53741008` | Coronary arteriosclerosis (disorder) |
| **Diagnosis** | `414545008` | Ischemic heart disease (disorder) |
| **Diagnosis** | `413838009` | Double vessel coronary artery disease (disorder) |
| **Diagnosis** | `413839001` | Triple vessel coronary artery disease (disorder) |
| **Procedure** | `232717009` | Coronary artery bypass grafting (procedure) |
| **Procedure** | `415070008` | Percutaneous coronary intervention (procedure) |
| **Procedure** | `36969009` | Placement of stent in coronary artery (procedure) |
| **Procedure** | `11101003` | Percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (procedure) |

---

## Execution Strategy

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Query 1  (primary)                                      │
│  Patient?_has:Condition...&_has:Procedure...             │
│  → Server AND-enforced → TRUE intersection result set    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼  If server does not support dual _has:
┌─────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Query 2            │  ∩  │  Query 3                    │
│  Condition leg      │     │  Procedure leg              │
│  patient IDs → Set A│     │  patient IDs → Set B        │
└─────────────────────┘     └─────────────────────────────┘
         └──────────── CLIENT-SIDE A ∩ B ────────────────┘
```

**Key design notes:**
- Both `_has` parameters in Query 1 are **ANDed** by the FHIR specification — a Patient is returned only if it satisfies **both** reverse-reference conditions simultaneously, eliminating false positives from diagnosis-only or procedure-only records.
- `clinical-status=active` and `verification-status=confirmed` on the Condition leg suppress problem list entries that were ruled out or are historical proposals.
- `status=completed` on the Procedure leg prevents procedure-ordering or screening-documentation entries from matching, directly addressing the sparse-procedure false-positive risk you noted.
- Queries 2 and 3 serve dual purposes: client-side fallback intersection, and detailed record retrieval for downstream chart review or audit.
1-expert.prompt_text: I need to identify patients who have BOTH a coded coronary heart disease diagnosis AND a documented coronary revascularization procedure — the interventional-management subset of CHD. On the diagnosis side, Condition resources should cover the SNOMED CT concepts for coronary arteriosclerosis, ischemic heart disease, and multi-vessel variants. On the procedure side, Procedure resources should cover coronary artery bypass grafting, percutaneous coronary intervention, coronary stent placement, and balloon angioplasty in SNOMED CT. The query should require both criteria simultaneously — patients who have only a diagnosis or only a procedure are excluded. Note that Procedure resources tend to be sparser than Condition resources in real EHRs, so combining both resource types is essential to avoid false positives from procedure codes that occasionally appear in screening contexts.

## FHIR Queries — Secondary Prevention Medications for Coronary Heart Disease

### 🩸 Antiplatelet Agents (Aspirin, Clopidogrel)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1191,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|32968&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 💊 High-Intensity Statins (Atorvastatin, Simvastatin)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|83367,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36567&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### ❤️ Beta Blockers (Carvedilol)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|20352&status=active&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### 📋 Parameter Reference

| Drug Class | Medication | RxNorm Code |
|---|---|---|
| Antiplatelet | Aspirin | `1191` |
| Antiplatelet | Clopidogrel | `32968` |
| Statin | Atorvastatin | `83367` |
| Statin | Simvastatin | `36567` |
| Beta Blocker | Carvedilol | `20352` |

### Key Notes
- **Comma-separated codes** within the same `code=` parameter apply **OR logic** — returns any matching medication in that class
- `status=active` limits to **currently active prescriptions**
- `intent=order` filters for **clinical orders** (excludes proposals/plans)
- `_include=MedicationRequest:patient` pulls the **associated Patient resource** in the same bundle
- To search across all drug classes in a **single query**, chain all RxNorm codes in one `code=` parameter with commas

## FHIR Query — CHD Secondary Prevention Medications (Single Combined Request)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1191,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|32968,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|83367,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36567,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|20352
```

---

### Parameter Breakdown

| RxNorm Code | Drug | CHD Secondary Prevention Role | Cross-Indication Noise |
|---|---|---|---|
| `1191` | Aspirin | Antiplatelet — post-ACS/PCI | Primary prevention, analgesic, anti-inflammatory |
| `32968` | Clopidogrel | P2Y12 inhibitor — DAPT after stent/ACS | PAD, stroke prevention |
| `83367` | Atorvastatin | High-intensity statin — LDL reduction post-MI | General hyperlipidemia, primary prevention |
| `36567` | Simvastatin | Moderate-intensity statin — post-MI lipid mgmt | General hyperlipidemia, primary prevention |
| `20352` | Carvedilol | Beta-blocker — LV protection post-MI | Heart failure (reduced EF), hypertension |

---

### Key Design Notes

- **Comma-separated `code=` values** → FHIR OR logic; returns any record matching at least one code in a **single round trip**
- **`code` parameter** maps to `MedicationRequest.medicationCodeableConcept` (inline coded medications); supplement with a chained query if your server stores medications as external `Medication` references:
  ```
  MedicationRequest?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1191,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|32968,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|83367,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|36567,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|20352
  ```
- **Cross-indication caveat:** These are **ingredient-level** codes; results will include prescriptions written for *any* indication. To narrow to CHD context, post-filter on `reasonCode` / `reasonReference` pointing to ICD-10 `I25.*` (chronic IHD) or `I21.*` (AMI) diagnoses, or join to a `Condition` resource.
- Consider adding **`&status=active`** if only currently active prescriptions are relevant to your use case.

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Query — CHD Cohort Full Clinical Context (Single Round-Trip)

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|414545008,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25.10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I25.110&_revinclude=Condition:patient&_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient&_revinclude=Procedure:patient
```

---

## Parameter Breakdown

| Parameter | Role | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| `_has:Condition:patient:code=...` | **Cohort filter** — reverse chain into Condition | Only return Patients who *have* a linked CHD Condition |
| `http://snomed.info/sct\|53741008` | Coronary arteriosclerosis (CHD) | Primary SNOMED CT CHD code |
| `http://snomed.info/sct\|414545008` | Ischemic heart disease | Broader SNOMED CT CHD concept |
| `http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm\|I25.10` | Atherosclerotic heart disease, native artery | ICD-10-CM CHD (no angina) |
| `http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm\|I25.110` | Atherosclerotic heart disease with unstable angina | ICD-10-CM CHD (with angina) |
| `_revinclude=Condition:patient` | Pull **all** Conditions for matched patients | Full problem list / comorbidities |
| `_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient` | Pull **all** MedicationRequests for matched patients | Current & historical prescriptions |
| `_revinclude=Procedure:patient` | Pull **all** Procedures for matched patients | Interventions (e.g., PCI, CABG) |

---

## What the Bundle Returns

```
Bundle
├── Patient/*          ← Patients with a CHD diagnosis
├── Condition/*        ← ALL conditions per patient (full problem list)
├── MedicationRequest/*← ALL medication requests per patient
└── Procedure/*        ← ALL procedures per patient (e.g., stenting, bypass)
```

---

## Key Design Notes

- **Comma-separated codes** on `_has:...:code` act as **OR** — a patient qualifies if they have *any* of the listed CHD codes
- `_has` performs the **reverse chain** filter without requiring a separate Condition query first
- All three `_revinclude` parameters are resolved **server-side in one HTTP request** — true single round-trip
- Add `&_count=50` or `&_page-token=...` for pagination on large CHD cohorts
- Append `&clinical-status=active` inside the `_has` chain to restrict to active diagnoses only (server-dependent support)
1-broad.prompt_text: Find Patient resources who have a coronary heart disease Condition, and use FHIR `_revinclude` to also return their MedicationRequest, Condition, and Procedure resources in the same bundle. This is one round-trip retrieval of full clinical context for a CHD cohort.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Comprehensive CHD Patient Bundle

The most effective single-request strategy is a **Patient-centric search** using `_has` (reverse chaining) to filter patients by coded CHD conditions, combined with `_revinclude` to pull all referenced clinical resources into one bundle. This avoids the limitation where `_revinclude` only applies to the primary resource set.

---

### Primary Query — Full CHD Patient Bundle

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|414545008,http://snomed.info/sct|414024009,http://snomed.info/sct|413838009&_revinclude=Condition:patient&_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient&_revinclude=Procedure:patient
```

---

### Supplementary Query — Explicit `subject` Parameter Variant
*(for servers that resolve `Condition.subject` under `subject` rather than `patient`)*

```
Patient?_has:Condition:subject:code=http://snomed.info/sct|53741008,http://snomed.info/sct|414545008,http://snomed.info/sct|414024009,http://snomed.info/sct|413838009&_revinclude=Condition:subject&_revinclude=MedicationRequest:subject&_revinclude=Procedure:subject
```

---

### Component Breakdown

| Layer | Mechanism | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `_has:Condition:patient:code=…` | Reverse chain filter | Selects only Patients who have ≥1 matching CHD Condition |
| `_revinclude=Condition:patient` | Reverse include | Attaches **all** Conditions for each matched Patient |
| `_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient` | Reverse include | Attaches **all** MedicationRequests for each matched Patient |
| `_revinclude=Procedure:patient` | Reverse include | Attaches **all** Procedures for each matched Patient |

---

### SNOMED CT Code Rationale

| SNOMED CT Code | Concept | CHD Category |
|---|---|---|
| `53741008` | Coronary arteriosclerosis (disorder) | Coronary arteriosclerosis |
| `414545008` | Ischemic heart disease (disorder) | Ischemic heart disease |
| `414024009` | Double vessel coronary artery disease (disorder) | Multi-vessel variant |
| `413838009` | Chronic ischemic heart disease (disorder) | Ischemic / multi-vessel overlap |

---

### Bundle Entry Composition

The returned `searchset` Bundle will contain entries of three types:

```
search.mode = "match"   → Patient resources (CHD-identified patients)
search.mode = "include" → Condition, MedicationRequest, Procedure resources
                          (all clinical context for matched patients)
```

---

### Notes

- **Code OR logic**: Comma-separated values in `_has:…:code` apply OR semantics — a patient is matched if any one condition code applies.
- **Iterative includes**: If your server supports `_revinclude:iterate`, you can extend the query to pull nested resources (e.g., `Encounter` referenced by Procedures).
- **Pagination**: Large cohorts should add `_count=50` and follow `Bundle.link[relation=next]` for paging.
- **Active filter**: Append `_has:Condition:patient:clinical-status=active` if you want to restrict to currently active CHD diagnoses only.
1-expert.prompt_text: I need a comprehensive patient bundle for CHD patients that includes not just the matching patients but also their associated clinical resources pulled in a single request. Start by identifying patients with a coded CHD diagnosis in Condition resources — covering SNOMED CT concepts for coronary arteriosclerosis, ischemic heart disease, and multi-vessel variants. Then use reverse-include parameters to attach all related Condition, MedicationRequest, and Procedure resources that reference those patients, so the bundle contains both the matched Patient entries and their full clinical context for downstream analysis.

### `_revinclude` Mechanics
- `_revinclude=MedicationRequest:patient` → all `MedicationRequest` resources whose `MedicationRequest.patient` points to a matched Patient
- `_revinclude=Condition:patient` → all `Condition` resources (full problem list, not just the CHD dx)
- `_revinclude=Procedure:patient` → all `Procedure` resources (CABG, PCI, stents, etc.)

The server returns a single `searchset` Bundle with `Patient` entries tagged `match` and all included resources tagged `include`.
